title: GarageHUD Work-List — the scoreboard
purpose: The single durable plan of record. Completed work is kept with its evidence (commit + test delta) so it can't fade; open work is kept with explicit completion and testing criteria so skipped work has nowhere to hide. This file is the source of truth for what is done and what is next — not memory, not the commit log alone.

session_protocol._note: Every session reads this file FIRST, before touching code. Follow these steps in order.
steps: 1. Read this whole file. The scoreboard, not recollection, defines the current state.

2. Choose the next item: the first item (top-to-bottom) whose status is 'incomplete' AND whose every depends_on id is 'complete' AND which is not 'blocked'. Honor an explicit user request over this default, but never silently reorder.
3. Set that item's status to 'in_progress' before starting work.
4. Do the work until EVERY string in completion_criteria is objectively met.
5. Run the tests named in testing_criteria (default: the full suite). Work is not done until they pass.
6. Record evidence in result: the test delta (e.g. '339 -> 342, +3'), the commit hash, the date (YYYY-MM-DD), and any deliberate trade-off. Then set status to 'complete'.

7. Never set 'complete' without a recorded, passing test result. Partial or unverified work stays 'incomplete', or 'blocked' with a blocked_reason. No exceptions — 'main is always green' (see CONTRIBUTING.md).
8. If new work is discovered, add it as a new item with status 'incomplete' rather than doing it silently.

status_values.incomplete: Not started, or started and not yet verified complete. The default for all new work.
status_values.blocked: Cannot proceed; blocked_reason states why (e.g. needs real hardware, awaiting owner decision).
status_values.complete: All completion_criteria met and testing_criteria passed, with evidence recorded in result.

conventions.test_command: cd GarageHUDKit && swift build 2>&1 | grep -cE 'error:|warning:'  # must print 0  &&  swift test  # all pass
conventions.green_bar: Zero warnings (-strict-concurrency=complete) and zero failing tests before any commit.
conventions.device_deploy: xcodebuild -project GarageHUD-iOS/GarageHUD.xcodeproj -scheme GarageHUD -configuration Release -destination 'platform=iOS,id=00008130-001228121120001C' -allowProvisioningUpdates build ; then xcrun devicectl device install app --device 00008130-001228121120001C <app>
conventions.roles: Vector (AI) does all build/test/install/commit/sync. Tim unlocks the phone and pushes. Never hand Tim multi-step click instructions.

items.title: Investment total reads the honest figure (max of live parts vs documented)
items.why: A frozen build-sheet lump sum silently ignored part-price edits; the first fix overshot so partial pricing understated the total (pricing one wheel could collapse a $25k build to $150).
completion_criteria: totalInvested = max(itemizedPartsCost, documentedTotalInvestment ?? 0)
investmentIsLiveFromParts true only when priced parts meet/exceed the documented figure
documentedReconcileFigure and pricedPartsSoFar drive honest copy across Specs, build-sheet export, and Steward grounding
testing_criteria: VehicleModelTests, GaragePersistenceTests, BuildSheetExporterTests, StewardGroundingTests updated and green
full suite green

result.notes: Open trade-off tracked as W-008: when documented > priced, the headline holds at documented until parts catch up.

items.title: Coherence pass — honest voice copy, tappable fleet Steward rows, Specs consolidation
items.why: Honesty is the app's pitch; stale 'voice on the way' copy, a fabricated spoken HP figure, read-only Steward rows, and an 8-panel Specs screen all eroded it.
completion_criteria: No fabricated numbers in voice test
Garage Steward rows navigate to the subject car
Specs is one calm Numbers section
testing_criteria: full suite green

items.title: Phase 3 — the Build Plan (intent layer)
items.why: The app knew what a car is and what's off now, but not where it's headed.
completion_criteria: BuildGoal on Vehicle
BuildPlanner orders wishlist parts support/sequence/power
Dashboard panel + goal editor
advisory on boost-without-fueling
testing_criteria: BuildPlanTests cover ordering, sequence tagging, advisory, goal persistence
full suite green

items.title: Wheel-honest build-goal progress
items.why: Progress compared a crank factory rating to a wheel target, overstating % to goal for un-dynoed cars.
completion_criteria: currentWheelHorsepowerEstimate (latest wheel dyno, else stock wheel baseline; never crank)
BuildProgress and the goal advisory use it
testing_criteria: BuildPlanTests: un-dynoed car with a wheel goal is not falsely 'at goal'
full suite green

items.title: Shareable sheets export as named files, not bare strings
items.why: Sharing a bare String gave the share sheet no filename, so 'Save to Files' fell back to stale clipboard data.
completion_criteria: SharableTextFile exports a titled .txt and plain text
build sheet and OBD report routed through it
testing_criteria: BuildSheetExporterTests cover file naming/sanitization
full suite green

items.title: Export-icon vocabulary — share arrow = sheet, archivebox = backup
items.why: The garage JSON backup wore the same share-arrow glyph as the build sheet, so it read as 'share this car' and handed over raw JSON.
completion_criteria: Both garage-backup exports use archivebox + 'restore file' preview
share arrow reserved for human-readable sheets
testing_criteria: full suite green

items.title: Whole-fleet sheet as a polished PDF
items.why: The only whole-garage export was JSON; owners need a human-readable document to hand a buyer or shop.
completion_criteria: FleetSheet renders the garage from HUDTheme to a single-page PDF
measured-vs-estimated graded
dot legend for recipients
shared from the garage share arrow
failed render throws instead of emitting a 0-byte file
testing_criteria: FleetSheetTests: valid PDF, empty fleet, full 8-bay render within CGContext limits
full suite green

result.notes: Visually inspected the rendered PDF before shipping.

items.title: Tune the cost-precedence threshold against real cars
items.why: When the documented total exceeds priced parts, the headline holds at documented until parts catch up — deliberate anti-understatement, but it means a single part edit won't move the headline in that window. Confirm this feels right on Tim's actual S2K/Fozzy data, or introduce a coverage threshold.
completion_criteria: Decision recorded: keep max() as-is OR switch to a coverage rule (e.g. parts-live once priced parts cover N% of installed parts)
behavior matches Tim's expectation on his real fleet
testing_criteria: any rule change covered by VehicleModelTests
full suite green

result.notes: Decision (Tim authorized 'GO'): keep totalInvested = max(itemized, documented). It's correct for Tim's real fleet (documented ≈ itemized, so parts drive it and edits move it), and the one soft spot — when documented > priced, the headline holds at documented until parts catch up — is already made transparent on Specs by the '$X of it priced so far' line. No coverage-threshold refinement needed. Revisit only if a real car's headline ever feels wrong.

items.title: Validate fleet-sheet sharing on a real device
items.why: The PDF render is unit-tested and eyeballed, but AirDrop/Files/Mail behavior, preview thumbnail, and large-fleet feel are unverified on hardware.
completion_criteria: Share the fleet sheet to Files, Mail, and AirDrop on the device; confirm a titled .pdf opens correctly
confirm the share-sheet preview reads well
testing_criteria: manual on-device check; note results here

result.on_device: Tim confirmed 2026-07-16: 'export to pdf works great, just as mocked up.'
verified_on_host: The exact bytes SharableFleetSheet exports (FleetSheetPDF.data) were written to 'GarageHUD Fleet Sheet.pdf' and open: macOS reports com.adobe.pdf and QuickLook renders it.
PDFKit: 1 page, 612x1271pt (US-Letter width, well under the 14,400pt CGContext ceiling), with 1003 chars of SELECTABLE vector text — crisp and searchable, not a rasterized image.
Rendered page reflects current honest logic: documented-total labeling (W-001), the dot legend, and goal %.
result.note: Stale 'remaining_on_device' checklist removed 2026-07-17 — superseded by Tim's on-device confirmation above.

items.title: Surface the first-run 'download a better voice' nudge
items.why: needsBetterVoiceDownload is wired but never shown, so a user with only the default robotic voice isn't guided to the Premium download (TD-007).
completion_criteria: A calm first-run nudge appears when only a default voice is installed and cloud voice is off
dismissible, non-nagging
testing_criteria: logic covered where testable; manual on-device confirm
full suite green

result.notes: VoiceNudge (pure, tested) gates the nudge: shows only when the device has just the robotic default voice, cloud voice is off, and it hasn't been dismissed; dismissal persists in UserDefaults so it never nags. Surfaced as a calm, dismissible banner in Ask Steward (where the voice is first heard) with a 'Voice settings' button and the iOS enhanced-voice download path. Gating unit-tested; the banner only appears on a device in the robotic-only state, which can't be forced on Tim's phone (his has enhanced voices), so visual appearance is condition-gated by the tested rule rather than eyeballed.

items.title: OBD adapter pairing UI
items.why: Handshake, profile model, and known-peripheral reconnect exist and are tested, but there's no UI to present discovered adapters, pick one, and persist the profile (TD-004).
completion_criteria: Present discovered ELM327 candidates
owner picks one; profile persisted
knownPeripheralID loaded on next launch
testing_criteria: view-model logic unit-tested where possible
full suite green

result.notes: Reconciled with the other dev's Jul-13 prior art (a6f65ae scan-first picker) instead of my from-scratch abstraction (dropped OBDPairing/MockOBDPairingService). Their architecture — scan-first hooks on OBDLiveDataSource reusing its proven bring-up — is better and I adopted it. Added on main's evolved base: catalog-awareness (OBDAdapterCandidate.isReachableOverBLE/unreachableReason so an MFi MX+ is a shown dead-end, not an inviting tap) and a persistence fix (record impliedSelection on pick so the saved profile is accepted next launch via acceptsSavedProfile). Picker rendered and visually verified. On-device BLE scan/validate is exercised in W-014 (blocked on hardware).

items.title: Version the CloudKit payload
items.why: The local file is a versioned document, but the CloudKit graph is still an unversioned bare array; version it before any non-additive change to the synced schema (TD-005 remaining, relates TD-001).
completion_criteria: CloudKit payload carries a schemaVersion
legacy bare-array payloads migrate in place
testing_criteria: round-trip + migration tests for the synced payload
full suite green

result.notes: CloudKit payload now uses the same versioned Document {schemaVersion, vehicles} envelope as the local file (CloudSyncManager.encodePayload/decodePayload reuse GaragePersistence). Pull tolerates both the versioned form and a legacy bare array, so older cloud records are never dropped. CloudPayloadTests covers round-trip, legacy tolerance, unreadable->nil. Real CloudKit round-trip cannot be unit-tested; the payload envelope is. One-time transition caveat documented in PERSISTENCE.md; TD-005 remaining now resolved.

items.title: On-device validation: cloud voice, LLM Steward, speech-in
items.why: Cloud TTS, the Foundation Models LLM path, and SFSpeechRecognizer are all gated and tested in logic but never exercised on real hardware/OS (TD-007).
completion_criteria: Cloud voice end-to-end with a real key (latency, audio-session/mic interplay, cost)
LLM path on an Apple-Intelligence device honors confidence bands and refuses out-of-record questions
speech-in works against a real mic
testing_criteria: manual on-device validation; record findings and any TD updates here
items.blocked_reason: Needs an OpenAI API key (Tim enters it directly into the app — Vector never handles keys) AND an Apple-Intelligence device (iPhone 15 Pro+/iOS 26) with a physical mic. Logic is built and gated; only real-hardware/credential validation remains.

items.title: OBD hardware validation
items.why: Real-adapter integration, ISO-TP/multi-ECU headers, and live disconnect/reconnect are unverified against physical hardware (TD-004). Synthetic transcript replay is done.
completion_criteria: Connect to a real ELM327 and decode live PIDs
handle a multi-ECU/ISO-TP response
survive a disconnect/reconnect cycle
testing_criteria: on-device/hardware validation; record findings here

items.title: DD-001 — eye-gravity review of every major screen
items.why: Design debt: each major screen should be reviewed for where the eye lands first; if two reviewers disagree, the hierarchy isn't finished.
completion_criteria: Each major screen reviewed for first-glance focus
any hierarchy fixes landed or logged as follow-up items
testing_criteria: design review (no automated test); record conclusions here

result.notes: First-reviewer eye-gravity pass: Garage reviewed from a live Simulator screenshot, other screens from code. Findings recorded in docs/DESIGN_DEBT.md (DD-001). Landed F1 (clear fix): the fleet-health strip clipped SERVICE DUE behind a non-obvious horizontal scroll; now wraps to two rows via ViewThatFits, verified on device before/after. F2 (power competes with state on an out-of-service car), F3 (active car shown twice), F4 (top-right bay-tab/arrow crowding), and a Dashboard condition-summary idea are logged for Tim as second reviewer (DD-001 rule: if two reviewers disagree, hierarchy is not finished).

items.title: Model vehicle purchase price (acquisition cost), distinct from build investment
items.why: GarageHUD has no purchase-price field — the only money fields are build/mod spend (documentedTotalInvestment, part costs). For a mostly-stock utility truck like the Tundra, what you paid is the primary money fact and it has nowhere to live, so Tim reasonably put '60K purchase price' in a free-text Note, which never becomes a number. Notes are prose; the figure can't surface.
completion_criteria: Vehicle gains a purchasePrice: Double? (tolerant decode)
Editable on Specs and shown as its own figure (not silently folded into 'Total Invested', which stays build/mod spend)
DECISION PENDING (Tim): whether to also show a combined 'all-in' (purchase + build) line, and whether purchase price appears on the fleet sheet
Honest wording: 'Purchase price' / 'paid', separate from 'invested'
testing_criteria: VehicleModelTests: purchasePrice round-trips and does not alter totalInvested
full suite green

result.notes: Vehicle.purchasePrice added (tolerant decode, older files -> nil). Shown as its own 'Purchase Price' line under a new OWNERSHIP section on Specs, editable, kept out of totalInvested. Separate lines, no all-in. Fleet-sheet inclusion deferred.

items.decision: Tim chose (2026-07-16): separate lines (no all-in combined line). Fleet-sheet inclusion deferred unless asked.

items.title: Maintenance / service cost tracking
items.why: Service records (oil changes, tire rotations) had no cost field, so lifetime maintenance spend couldn't be tracked — the other half of why Tim's Tundra costs ended up in a note. Requested alongside W-016.
completion_criteria: BuildEvent gains cost: Double? (service occurrence carries what it cost)
A service record's cost is editable in Service History
Vehicle.serviceSpend sums service-record costs; shown as its own line on Specs, separate from Total Invested and Purchase price
Total service spend surfaced in the Service History view
testing_criteria: VehicleModelTests: BuildEvent.cost round-trips; serviceSpend sums; markMaintenanceDone can carry a cost; totalInvested unaffected
full suite green

result.notes: BuildEvent.cost added; markMaintenanceDone takes an optional cost; setBuildEventCost prices/clears a record after the fact. Editable per record in Service History (tap 'Add cost'), with a running TOTAL SERVICE SPEND header. Vehicle.serviceSpend shown as its own line on Specs, separate from Total Invested and Purchase price. Follow-up (Tim): 'Mark done' now prompts for the cost via an optional alert field — Log works with it blank, so the prompt never restricts the record.

items.title: F2: dim POWER on an out-of-service car (DD-001 eye-gravity)
items.why: On a torn-down car the bright cyan POWER (e.g. 477 WHP) out-competed the amber OUT OF SERVICE and red TUNE STATE for the eye — power isn't the story when the engine is apart.
completion_criteria: Hero instrument rail shows POWER in textPrimary (not cyan) while serviceStatus.isInService
Cyan retained when the car is in service
testing_criteria: full suite green
visually verified in simulator (out-of-service hero)

result.notes: POWER cell in the hero instrument rail now renders textPrimary (plain white) while serviceStatus.isInService, cyan otherwise. Verified in the simulator: on the out-of-service S2K the eye now lands on OUT OF SERVICE / TUNE STATE HOLD / NEXT ACTION rather than the dyno number. F3 (active car shown twice) and F4 (bay-tab/arrow crowding) remain logged in DESIGN_DEBT.md for Tim.

items.title: F3: active car not shown twice on the garage
items.why: The spotlighted (active-bay) car appeared as both the hero and the first grid card below it.
completion_criteria: Grid omits the spotlighted vehicle (LazyVGrid reflows); the fleet map shows the rest of the garage
testing_criteria: full suite green
visually verified in simulator

result.notes: Verified: with S2K spotlighted, the grid now leads with FOZZY, not a second S2K card.

items.title: F4: bay label vs open-arrow collision on the hero
items.why: VehicleVisualFallback rendered 'BAY N' top-right, the exact corner the spotlight overlays its open-arrow button — they overlapped.
completion_criteria: 'BAY N' moved to the top-left of the hero art, clear of the top-right arrow, in both the garage spotlight and the per-car detail hero
testing_criteria: full suite green
visually verified in simulator

result.notes: Verified: BAY 1 now sits top-left, arrow alone top-right, no overlap.

items.title: Honesty audit: a numberless dyno was labeled measured
items.why: Adversarial audit — a .dyno record with no wheelHorsepower made currentHorsepowerEstimate fall back to the CRANK factory figure while 'measured' was gated on record TYPE, so the build sheet and fleet sheet (both shareable) stated e.g. '155 whp measured' for a car that never measured wheel power. Crank shown as wheel, estimate shown as measured. Adjacent to W-004, which fixed only the Build-Plan number and left the label paths leaky.
completion_criteria: measuredWheelHorsepower + hasMeasuredPower on Vehicle (a real dyno number is the only thing that counts as measured)
currentHorsepowerEstimate = measuredWheelHorsepower ?? factory (no crank-via-numberless-dyno, no shadowing an earlier real dyno)
every measured label gated on hasMeasuredPower, not record type (build sheet, fleet sheet, BuildProgress, dashboard torque)
testing_criteria: HonestyInvariantsTests: numberless dyno never labeled measured; earlier real dyno not shadowed; installed part overrides confirmed-stock; planned part not counted as covered
full suite green

result.notes: Empirically confirmed the lie ('155 whp measured'), fixed, and locked with regression tests. Audit also cleared attacks 2 (installed part overrides stale stock claim) and 3 (planned part reads 'undocumented, planned', not 'covered') as honest. Attack 4 (absurd user-entered dyno reported raw) is garbage-in, not an engine lie — possible future sanity-flag, not tracked. UPDATE 2026-07-17: attack 4 was overruled by the Fable review (W-022) — a non-positive dyno figure no longer counts as a measurement.

items.title: Fable review fixes — six proven honesty lies, three integrity bugs
items.why: Independent adversarial review (Fable, 2026-07-17) empirically proved the app stating falsehoods: crank labeled 'whp' in BuildAssessment.powerSummary (third instance of the W-004/W-021 class); wishlist costs reported as spend on Specs and in LLM grounding; a 3,000-mi-overdue truck producing zero Steward observations ('Nothing stands out right now.') while the briefing header called it overdue; gap evidence denying a logged wishlist part; grounding denying a logged numberless dyno; '-50 whp (measured)' on the shareable build sheet. Plus: a fresh device could clobber the whole cloud garage (conflict guard skipped when appliedCloudUpdatedAt was nil); mark-done with unknown odometer kept a stale mileage baseline (fresh service read overdue); OBD boost hardcoded sea-level baro; moveParts carried the source car's installDate into the destination's history.
completion_criteria: Vehicle.measuredDynoRecords/latestMeasuredDyno is the single source for every measured-power claim; non-positive figures never count
spendByCategory counts installed parts only
Steward.observe judges maintenance mileage-aware, same as every other surface; evidence and build-sheet copy name the actual overdue driver
supportGap acknowledges planned parts; grounding acknowledges numberless dyno sessions and labels power-to-weight basis
pushNow treats never-applied-cloud-state as a conflict (snapshot + adopt) instead of blind-pushing; stripped-remote exception preserved
markMaintenanceDone clears the mileage baseline when the odometer is unknown
OBD decoder reports raw MAP kPa, polls baro PID 0133, computes gauge boost against measured baro
moveParts re-dates the moved part to the transfer moment
testing_criteria: HonestyInvariantsTests regression per lie; PartTransferTests re-date; MileageMaintenanceTests baseline-clear; OBDPIDDecoderTests baro/gauge
full suite green

result.notes: Remaining from the review, deliberately NOT done here (needs owner/architect judgment): a table-driven cross-surface honesty sweep test (adversarial vehicle matrix x every textual surface x global assertions) — the structural guard against instance #4 of the crank-as-wheel class; a PowerFigure/money-bucket type so value+label can't be mis-paired at call sites; odometer input validation; units/locale; a UI path to discover and restore conflict snapshots. UPDATE: Tim authorized continuing — tracked as W-023..W-026.

items.title: Cross-surface honesty sweep test
items.why: Three reviews found the same value/label mis-pair (crank as 'whp', estimate as 'measured') in three different places — point fixes don't stop instance #4; a quantified invariant does. Designing the sweep immediately caught a fourth live instance: the voice Steward's power answer said 'No dyno is logged yet' for a car with a numberless dyno session on record (the H5 denial, in a surface the grounding fix missed).
completion_criteria: HonestySweepTests renders every pure textual surface (build sheet, grounding, observations, assessment, next step, four voice replies, briefing, tune readiness, build plan) for an adversarial vehicle matrix

Global assertions: whp earned or estimate-framed; 'measured' earned or negated; the record never denied; planned money always framed planned; a bare car yields no numbers; surfaces agree on overdue-ness
Mutation-verified: reintroducing the W-022 crank-as-whp bug makes the sweep fail with a pointed message
StewardConversation numberless-dyno denial fixed (same wording as grounding)
testing_criteria: HonestySweepTests green; full suite green

result.tests: 376 -> 377, +1 (one sweep test, ~110 vehicle x surface x rule checks inside)
result.notes: New textual surfaces must be added to HonestySweepTests.surfaces to inherit coverage — noted in the test header. Fleet-sheet PDF is SwiftUI-rendered and not sweepable as text; its logic reads the same shared helpers.

items.title: PowerFigure — value and honest label paired in one type
items.why: The recurring crank-as-whp bug was structural: currentHorsepowerEstimate (which can be a crank rating) and hasMeasuredPower lived apart, and every surface re-paired them by hand. Wiring the type up found FOUR MORE live view-layer instances the text sweep can't reach: VehicleIdentitySurface, GarageSpotlightView (the garage hero rail printed a crank rating as 'WHP'), GarageOverviewView, and FleetComparisonView (crank and wheel figures mixed in one column under a shared 'whp' header).
completion_criteria: PowerFigure { value, isMeasured, unit, qualifier, compactLabel, labeled }; Vehicle.currentPowerFigure is the display source
BuildAssessment, BuildSheetExporter, FleetSheet, SpecSheet, VehicleIdentitySurface, GarageSpotlightView, GarageOverviewView render through it
FleetComparisonView POWER column wheel-normalized (currentWheelHorsepowerEstimate) so the shared 'whp' header is true for every row
SpecSheet 'Current' labels measured vs factory and uses the figure's unit
testing_criteria: HonestyInvariantsTests PowerFigure pairing test; HonestySweepTests still green over refactored surfaces
full suite green

result.notes: View-layer visual checks (spotlight rail, identity surface, comparison grid) still deserve Tim's on-device eyeball — copy lengths changed slightly ('240 HP' where 'XXX WHP' used to render).

items.title: Odometer sanity — entry-time anomaly warnings and a self-contradiction observation
items.why: The odometer is the reasoning spine (currentMileage, milesPerDay, every mileage due state and projection derive from event readings), yet any typo was accepted silently — one slipped digit (50,000 -> 580,000) poisoned all of it with no warning and no surfacing. The review ranked this the biggest unmodeled input risk.
completion_criteria: Vehicle.odometerAnomaly(proposing:on:) flags regressions (below an already-recorded reading of the same era) and implausible rates (> 1,000 mi/day implied), era-aware for backdated entries
AddEditBuildEventView shows a calm, NON-BLOCKING caution next to the mileage field (warn, never restrict the record); editing excludes the event under edit
Steward rule data.odometerRegression surfaces a record that already disagrees with itself (later date, lower reading) as a confirmed caution
testing_criteria: OdometerSanityTests: quiet pass, first-reading pass, regression, slipped digit, road-trip day not flagged, backdated-era consistency, Steward surfacing, consistent record quiet
full suite green

result.notes: MaintenanceEditorView's baseline field intentionally not gated — it sets a schedule baseline, not a biography reading. The caution never blocks: a genuine correction (replaced cluster, fixing an old typo) must always be loggable.

items.title: Recovery — discover, export, restore preserved garage files
items.why: GarageHUD's data-safety story wrote conflict snapshots and unreadable-file backups to disk, but nothing ever listed them — the notices only showed in the session that created them, so a preserved file was effectively lost the moment the app restarted. A safety file the owner can't find is not safety.
completion_criteria: GarageStore.recoverySnapshots lists conflict snapshots, pre-restore copies, and unreadable-file backups with dates and decoded vehicle counts
GarageStore.restore(from:) replaces the garage undoably — the current garage is preserved first as a pre-restore snapshot; an unreadable file is refused and left untouched
RecoveryView (sheet from a Garage-overview recovery row shown whenever preserved files exist): export, confirm-to-restore, swipe-to-delete
restore failure surfaces an alert, never silently no-ops
testing_criteria: RecoverySnapshotTests: listing with counts, restore + pre-restore round-trip (restore the restore), unreadable refused/untouched, delete
full suite green; iOS simulator app build green

result.notes: Restore is user-confirmed in UI and always undoable at the store level. Recovery row placement (garage overview, under data-safety notices) deserves Tim's eye for DD-001 gravity; it only appears when preserved files exist.

items.title: InvestmentFigure — money headline and source wording paired in one type
items.why: The 'total invested' label logic (logged-parts vs documented, reconcile figure, priced-so-far, the three-way Specs explanation) was re-derived by hand on five surfaces (build sheet, grounding, fleet sheet, Specs, voice) — the same mis-pair structure that produced eight crank-as-whp instances, waiting to happen to money.
completion_criteria: InvestmentFigure { total, isLiveFromParts, documentedReconcile, pricedSoFar } with every wording variant (sourceShort/sourceLong/sheetPhrase/spokenVerb/explanation) defined once
Vehicle.investmentFigure nil when nothing invested; all five surfaces refactored onto it
testing_criteria: HonestyInvariantsTests source-wording pairing test
full suite green

items.title: Fleet-sheet card as a pure, sweep-covered model
items.why: The fleet sheet is the buyer-facing artifact, but as SwiftUI it was the one honesty-critical surface the cross-surface sweep (W-023) could not check. Extracting its words into FleetSheetCardModel closes that hole: the sweep checks the model, the card only inks it.
completion_criteria: FleetSheetCardModel.make(for:) produces every string the card prints (power value/caption/gain, investment, stock baseline, goal, subsystem statuses, headline)
FleetSheetCard renders exclusively from the model (documented rule: a string added directly to the card bypasses the sweep)
sweepText added to HonestySweepTests.surfaces
testing_criteria: FleetSheetCardModel honesty test; HonestySweepTests green over the new surface; FleetSheetTests (PDF render) green
full suite green; iOS simulator build green

result.notes: Rendered output unchanged by construction (strings extracted verbatim); PDF render test still green. Worth one visual diff of the exported sheet on device.

items.title: Non-positive figures gated in record summaries and dashboard torque
items.why: PerformanceRecord.summary printed any entered wheelHorsepower ('-50 whp') on record rows and the garage overview; the dashboard torque stat had the same hole for wheelTorque. Same gate as measuredDynoRecords: a non-positive figure is not a measurement.
completion_criteria: PerformanceRecord.summary falls back to the type name for non-positive dyno figures
Dashboard torque stat ignores non-positive wheelTorque
testing_criteria: HonestyInvariantsTests summary gating test
full suite green

items.title: Evidence tap-through — every headline figure answers 'why should I trust this?'
items.why: The Constitution demands 'never pretend certainty, always explain' and the model has carried provenance all along, but no surface let the owner inspect it — trust-by-assertion instead of trust-by-evidence. Also the discoverability principle: evidence inspectable in place, not buried.
completion_criteria: ProvenanceBuilder (pure) explains power (dyno + date + location, stale-hardware disclosure, factory rating, stock-wheel baseline with assumed-loss disclosure), investment (both max() inputs, which leads and why, planned money disclosed as excluded), and odometer (source event, learned rate, self-disagreement disclosure)
ProvenanceTappable popover on Specs: Current power, Total Invested, and a new Odometer stat under OWNERSHIP (Specs previously had no odometer line)
All three provenance texts are HonestySweepTests surfaces
testing_criteria: FigureProvenanceTests: measured/unmeasured power incl. staleness + assumption disclosure, investment max-rule, odometer source/rate/self-disagreement, nil-when-no-figure
full suite green; iOS simulator build green

result.notes: Also updated DESIGN_DEBT.md DD-001: F2/F3/F4 recorded as landed (W-018..W-020) with Tim's on-device power-label confirmation; Dashboard condition-summary idea stays open. Popover visual (info glyph placement, card width) deserves Tim's eye on device.

items.title: Vehicle Biography export — the whole story, exportable
items.why: 'The story is the product' (Constitution); FUTURE.md's Vehicle Biography Export. The build sheet is the spec-card; the biography is the life: full timeline, every service with its cost, performance history graded by evidence, the three ownership money facts kept separate, and a provenance appendix (W-030) explaining where each headline figure comes from. The document for a sale, an insurance claim, a valuation, or a legacy.
completion_criteria: BiographyExporter.text/file: identity, power + full performance record, build by category with dated costs, planned section (framed as planned), full service record with costs + total, complete timeline with odometer stamps, ownership (purchase / build investment / maintenance spend, never conflated), provenance appendix
Shared from Specs ('Share vehicle biography', book glyph) alongside the build sheet
Biography text is an HonestySweepTests surface
testing_criteria: BiographyExporterTests: whole-story content, un-dynoed car never reads measured, file naming
full suite green; iOS simulator build green

result.notes: Text document first (same SharableTextFile route as the build sheet). A polished PDF rendition via the FleetSheet ImageRenderer pattern is a natural follow-up once Tim confirms the content reads right — logged as the next candidate, not silently started.

items.title: Typed rule ids — emitters and parsers share StewardRuleID
items.why: Rule ids are load-bearing strings: StewardResolution routes 'Mark serviced' / 'Confirm stock' buttons by parsing them, and NextStep branches on them. Emitters minted ids with inline literals while parsers re-derived the format with prefix surgery — a format change on either side broke the other silently.
completion_criteria: StewardRuleID defines every fixed id and parameterized builder/parser pair (gap, maintenance, fleetSharedGap, pullFlagged) in one place
Steward, StewardFleet, StewardResolution, StewardNextStep all read from it; no inline rule-id literals remain at emit/parse sites
data.odometerRegression gains a resolution ('Review the timeline') so the W-025 observation is actionable
testing_criteria: StewardRuleIDTests: gap round-trip for every category, maintenance round-trip, emitted-observation-to-resolution end-to-end
full suite green

items.title: Dashboard condition line — what matters now, never below the fold
items.why: DD-001's remaining Dashboard finding: on a busy car 'the condition' can fall below the fold. One calm NEXT line directly under the identity, fed by Steward.nextStep (an existing, tested judgment — no new claims).
completion_criteria: A single 'NEXT — <action>' line under VehicleIdentitySurface, shown only when the Steward has a next step
testing_criteria: nextStep already covered by StewardNextStepTests; visual gravity check is Tim's on device
full suite green; iOS simulator build green

result.tests: 405 green (no new logic — presentation of an existing judgment)
result.notes: DD-001 second-reviewer eye-gravity check on device is Tim's: the NEXT line should support, not compete with, the identity surface.

items.title: Biography as a styled PDF — one model, two inks
items.why: The biography is the buyer/legacy artifact; a styled PDF reads like GarageHUD where a .txt reads like a printout. Built on the W-028 principle: BiographyModel holds the words, the text export and the PDF document both render from it, so the two inks can never diverge and the sweep keeps covering the content.
completion_criteria: BiographyExporter refactored around BiographyModel { headerLines, sections, footer }; text output derives from it (existing tests/sweep unchanged)

BiographySheetDocument renders the model in HUDTheme; per-section 48-line cap with a DISCLOSED cut ('… and N more — the full history is in the text export') so a decade-long timeline stays inside the 14,400pt CGContext ceiling
SharableBiographySheet (deferred render, throws on empty) replaces the text file behind Specs' 'Share vehicle biography'
testing_criteria: BiographyPDFTests: valid non-trivial PDF, 400-event timeline renders within limits while the text export keeps the full history, bare car renders
full suite green; iOS simulator build green

result.notes: Visual polish of the rendered page (panel spacing, header scale) deserves Tim's eyeball on a real export, same as W-009 did for the fleet sheet.

items.title: Per-car share menu in the detail banner — documents reachable from every tab
items.why: Tim couldn't find the biography: both of a car's shareable documents lived at the very bottom of the scrolling Specs tab — the exact 'buried affordance' discoverability pattern the review flagged. A car-level export belongs at the car level.
completion_criteria: Share-arrow menu in the vehicle banner (visible on every tab): Vehicle biography (PDF) and Build sheet (text)
W-006 icon vocabulary preserved: share arrow = human-readable sheet
testing_criteria: full suite green; iOS simulator build green; placement is Tim's eye-gravity call on device

result.notes: Specs keeps its in-context share buttons too — reachable both where you're looking at the numbers and from anywhere on the car.

items.title: NEXT line is actionable — a step that names an action resolves in place
items.why: Tim tapped the W-033 NEXT line and nothing happened — a dead affordance that names an action, violating the app's own actionable-in-place principle. The resolution engine already existed; the line just wasn't wired to it.
completion_criteria: NextStep carries its source observation (advisory, gap-matched assessment step, stale tune, caution); nil only when the fix lives elsewhere on the same screen (rebuild checklist)
Tapping the NEXT line opens the source observation's resolution dialog (same flow as Steward rows), with a chevron affordance so it reads tappable; a non-resolvable step renders as a plain line
testing_criteria: StewardRuleIDTests: overdue step resolves to markServiced(item); assessment step carries an actionable gap observation
full suite green; installed to Tim's device

result.notes: Deployed to the iPhone (Release) immediately — this one came straight from Tim's thumb.

items.title: Every NEXT line acts on tap — full-row hit target, rebuild step scrolls to its checklist
items.why: Tim reported the NEXT line dead. Two real defects: (1) the W-036 carve-out left the out-of-service rebuild step as a plain line on the theory the checklist was 'right below' — it's four panels down, and an affordance tappable on one car but dead on another reads as broken; (2) the tappable rows had no contentShape, so only the text glyphs themselves were hit-testable — a thumb in the row's empty space did nothing even on an actionable car.
completion_criteria: contentShape(Rectangle()) — the whole row is the tap target
Resolvable steps open their resolution dialog (chevron.right); the rebuild step scrolls to the rebuild checklist via ScrollViewReader (chevron.down) — no NEXT line is ever inert
Accessibility hints name each behavior
testing_criteria: full suite green; deployed to Tim's device for his confirmation

result.notes: Awaiting Tim's on-device confirmation that the tap now lands on both the S2K (scroll) and an actionable car (dialog).

items.title: One gesture, one response — the NEXT line unified under Tim's standing rule (DD-004)
items.why: Tim, watching himself fight the NEXT line's affordance vocabulary (chevron-right dialog vs chevron-down scroll-jump, conditional tappability, three patch-deploy rounds): 'fundamental principle — excess motion, user confused, not intuitive… you would have seen me say fuck this app.' The scroll-jump and the two-behavior vocabulary were the violation, not the tap target.
completion_criteria: DD-004 recorded in DESIGN_DEBT.md as a standing rule: one gesture one response; never auto-scroll on tap; no conditional affordances; boring beats clever; batch UI deploys

NEXT line has exactly one behavior everywhere: tap opens the standard resolution dialog (the Steward-row pattern Tim already knows); the in-service step carries its observation so the out-of-service car resolves the same way ('Mark back in service')
Scroll-jump, ScrollViewReader wiring, and the chevron-down vocabulary removed; when a step has no resolvable source the line is not shown at all — never a dead control
testing_criteria: full suite green; single deploy to Tim's device for his read

result.notes: The principle is also saved to agent memory so future sessions inherit it. If the line still doesn't earn its place, the next move per DD-004 is removal, not another variant.

items.title: NEXT is information, not a control — the tappable versions retired
items.why: Tim walked the S2K flow: the line says 'Finish the engine teardown', the tap's only offered action was 'Mark back in service' — the OPPOSITE of the label — and confirming would clear his 5 open checklist items. Label and action cannot be guaranteed coherent across every step source, so per DD-004 (boring beats clever, prefer removal over teaching) the line stops acting entirely.
completion_criteria: conditionLine is a plain one-line summary: no Button, no chevron, no dialog — DD-001's original purpose only
Acting stays on the Steward rows, where each row's text matches its own resolution options
NextStep.source stays in the model (tested, used by rule-id round-trips) but no UI wires a tap to it
testing_criteria: full suite green; deployed to Tim's device

result.notes: Standing offer to Tim remains: if the line doesn't earn its place as pure information, removal is one commit. Lesson recorded under DD-004: a summary label must never be a door to an action that contradicts it, especially not one that clears user data two taps away.

items.title: NEXT line removed; Recovery explains itself
items.why: Tim's third strike on the NEXT line settled it: it duplicated the garage hero's existing NEXT ACTION rail fact in a different style one screen away (the color inconsistency he reported), and as pure information it named work while leaving him to scroll and hunt for the controls. DD-004 verdict: removal — next action has one home (the hero rail), acting has one home (the Steward rows). Separately, 'RECOVERY — 2 preserved garage files' with no explanation read as a warning; he had to ask what it meant.
completion_criteria: Dashboard conditionLine deleted; a comment marks the grave and the rule so it doesn't come back

Garage recovery row says what the files are in the same breath: 'automatic safety copies… Nothing is wrong — if your garage looks right, you can delete them'; RecoveryView header says the same before the list
testing_criteria: full suite green; single deploy to Tim's device

result.notes: Lesson attached to DD-004's ledger: before adding a surface, check whether the information already has a home — the dashboard NEXT line duplicated the hero rail from birth.

items.title: NEXT STEP carries its door — visible labeled verbs in the identity block
items.why: Tim, on the Fozzy: 'Address clutch/drivetrain is the open item… what do you want the user to do right here?' The honest answer was: nothing — the app gave no path from the instruction to any of its three intended responses (log the part / confirm stock / plan it), and for drivetrain there wasn't even a Steward row (gap rules only cover fueling/cooling/brakes). An instruction without a door. Tim approved the visible-buttons proposal.
completion_criteria: The pre-existing NEXT STEP block (VehicleIdentitySurface) shows its verbs as visible, labeled buttons — no hidden gesture, each button present exactly when its action exists (DD-004)
Assessment steps for categories without emitted gap rules (drivetrain, engine internals) synthesize an equivalent gap observation so the verbs exist for them too
Buttons route through the dashboard's existing perform(_:) — same machinery as the Steward rows (confirm-stock mutates in place; add-part goes to Parts; mark-serviced logs it)
The teardown step carries no verbs: its only in-place resolution would contradict its own words (W-039); its surface is the rebuild checklist
testing_criteria: StewardRuleIDTests: drivetrain step carries confirmStock/addPart verbs; teardown step carries none
full suite green; deployed to Tim's device

result.notes: Supersedes the W-038 note about the in-service step carrying its observation — reverted per W-039's lesson. Button styling (hairline capsules under the rationale) awaits Tim's eye.

items.title: Actionability sweep — 'an instruction must carry a door', mechanized
items.why: Tim asked why the initial review missed the Fozzy dead-end. Honest answer: the review audited claims, not experiences — it verified every string and walked zero flows, and attention followed the harness (string-truth was testable, flow-completeness wasn't). Same cure as the honesty class: make the invariant enforced instead of remembered.
completion_criteria: ActionabilitySweepTests: every non-informational observation resolves to at least one concrete action; every next step carries resolvable verbs or is the documented teardown exemption
Matrix triggers every observation family and next-step branch (incl. the W-041 non-gap-rule category case)
Mutation-verified: removing the synthesized gap observation fails the sweep with 'instruction without a door' messages
testing_criteria: full suite green

result.notes: Review-method lesson recorded: string-truth sweeps catch lies; this catches unanswerable asks. Future surfaces that emit instructions must register in one sweep or the other. No deploy needed (test-only).

items.title: No circular verbs — resolution options reflect the knowledge state
items.why: Tim: 'confirm drivetrain is factory-stock from fozzy page not resolving… additionally, the fozzy's drivetrain was marked as stock.' Exactly: the drivetrain was ALREADY confirmed stock, and the surface offered 'confirm it's stock' anyway — a circular verb whose tap is a no-op insert, so the surface never resolves. The verbs were blind to what the record already knew.
completion_criteria: StewardResolution gap options are knowledge-aware: a confirmedAbsent (stock-confirmed) system under load offers only 'Add the <system> upgrade' — never 'confirm stock' again (applies to Steward rows and the NEXT STEP block alike)
The synthesized gap observation states the truth for its state: confirmed-stock reads 'the factory <system> is confirmed in place… carrying the added load — worth weighing', not 'isn't documented'
ActionabilitySweep gains the general rule: no offered verb may set a state that is already set (circular-verb check), with a confirmed-stock-under-load car in the matrix
testing_criteria: full suite green; deployed to Tim's device

result.notes: Third law joins the sweeps: never say the unearned (honesty), never ask the unanswerable (actionability), never offer the already-done (circularity).

items.title: Driveline attention is owner-calibrated: 450 whp, not +40-whp-gain
items.why: Tim, on the Fozzy's clutch nag: 'you telling me a simple stage1 upgrade on a Subaru warrants a clutch and drivetrain, no way… I won't touch the transmission till over 450HP, wouldn't touch the clutch till then.' The trigger was an invented flat gain>=40whp — flagging a stage-1 factory-turbo car (~25% bump the factory driveline was engineered for) the same as a built car at double stock. A fourth blind-spot class: honest labels on domain-uncalibrated judgment rules. The review audited whether numbers were true, never whether the thresholds were.
completion_criteria: Vehicle.drivelineAttentionWheelHP = 450 (Tim's words quoted at the definition) with powerDemandsDrivelineAttention keyed on the wheel-honest current figure
Every powerUp gate reads it: BuildAssessment relevance (drivetrain, brakes), Steward brakes-gap rule (evidence now says 'Power is past 450 whp'), fleet shared-gap brakes, BuildPlanner brake priority
Suspension-upgrade brake trigger unchanged; engine-internals rule unchanged (FI + 80 whp gain)
Test fixtures updated to sit on the correct side of the line (480 whp where scrutiny is the point)
testing_criteria: full suite green; deployed to Tim's device

result.notes: Effect on the real fleet: the stage-1 Fozzy stops being told it needs a clutch; the 477-whp S2K remains correctly in scope. Open question deliberately NOT built without Tim: factory-forced-induction awareness (a factory-turbo car's fueling/cooling was engineered for boost — the FI-triggered support reasoning still assumes aftermarket boost). On the board when Tim wants it.

items.title: Factory-turbocharged awareness — a factory charger is the car, not a mod
items.why: Tim: 'teach the app this platform is boosted from the factory… it's a must to understand.' The reasoning treated forced induction as always-aftermarket: a factory-turbo car with no FI part logged got NO live boost envelope, a HOLD for daring to have a boost map, and — once anything triggered assessment — 'Forced induction is installed.' wording that isn't true of a stock charger.
completion_criteria: Vehicle.hasFactoryForcedInduction: owner override (Steward Inputs toggle) ?? inference from engine description ('turbo'/'supercharged') and known factory-boosted model markers (XT, WRX, STI, Evo, GTR, Mazdaspeed, Supra, SRT4)

Two gates, one definition each: runsBoost (any boost — live envelope defaults, boost maps legitimate in the tuner) vs runsElevatedBoost (support scrutiny — aftermarket FI, or factory platform with a calibration on record or a measured gain >= 25% over stock baseline)
A SHOWROOM-STOCK factory-turbo car gets: a live boost envelope, a legitimate boost map, and ZERO support-gap nags — the factory engineered that support
Gap trigger wording is honest per path: 'Forced induction is installed.' only for aftermarket; 'running a tune (Cobb Accessport)' / 'making well over its stock power' for factory platforms

Vehicle.calibrationTerms/calibrationPartOnRecord shared by the tuner's calibration check and the elevated-boost gate (one definition of 'a tune is on record'); grounding states 'factory turbocharged… not a modification' to the LLM
testing_criteria: FactoryBoostTests: inference/override, stock-stays-quiet, tuned-earns-scrutiny with honest wording, gain-only path, envelope default, boost-map-not-a-hold, Cobb recognized
factoryTurboTuned car added to the honesty sweep matrix; full suite green; deployed to Tim's device

result.notes: Effect on the real Fozzy (Cobb, bolt-ons, 381 whp E85): live boost envelope by default, boost map legitimate, fueling/cooling now honestly in scope because the tune turned the boost up — phrased as 'running a tune (Cobb Accessport)', never as installed FI. Tim is checking whether the fuel pump is already logged; if it is, the fueling check reads supported.

items.title: A statistic is not a task — cost-per-hp retired from the attention stream
items.why: Tim: 'One button always in my face is "this build cost $$$$ per horsepower" Resolve? … which doesn't make it go away.' The efficiency.costPerHp observation was permanent, unresolvable arithmetic sitting in a panel captioned 'Actions only when earned', mapped to a Resolve option (Log a dyno result) that couldn't change it. The dual of the actionability law: no door on something that isn't an ask.
completion_criteria: efficiency.costPerHp removed from Steward.observe (with a NOTE at the site so it doesn't come back) and from StewardResolution / StewardRuleID

The figure keeps its real homes, caveats intact: Specs (Cost/WHP with provenance tap), the grounding record ([Moderate — wheel-estimate, not dyno-corrected]), and the voice's efficiency answer when asked
DD-004 gains the law: 'A statistic is not a task'
Pinning tests repurposed to assert the figure at its home surfaces and its permanent absence from the attention stream
testing_criteria: full suite green; deployed to Tim's device

result.tests: 419 -> 418 (net -1: one statistic-rule test retired, replacements folded into repurposed tests)
result.notes: fleet.valueLeader is the same class at fleet level (informational stat, unresolvable) — it's rarer (needs 2+ dynoed cars) and un-actionable already, so left in place but WATCHED: if Tim reports it as noise, retire it the same way.

items.title: Biography goes paper-first — white ground, ink text, restrained accent
items.why: Tim: 'as nice as the biography is, don't you think Black is a touch background to print into?' Right: the document's natural habitat is print (buyer, insurance, service folder), and a cockpit-dark page is a full-bleed toner bath. The cockpit dark is the app's identity ON SCREEN; paper conventions win on paper.
completion_criteria: BiographySheetDocument renders on a PrintTheme: white paper, ink-dark text, HUD cyan darkened to a print-legible accent, light panels with hairline strokes
Fleet sheet deliberately left dark (Tim approved its screen look, W-009); lightening it is one commit away if asked
Content untouched — same BiographyModel words, honesty sweeps unaffected
testing_criteria: full suite green (PDF render tests pass); visual print check is Tim's on a real export

items.title: Steward threshold provenance audit (Fable concern #3)
items.why: An app whose whole pitch is 'never state a guess as a fact' had a reasoning layer full of invented constants (coolant/boost limits, sequence/quiet windows, drivetrain losses, pull-fit gates) presented with the same authority as facts. Only discovery process for a wrong one was 'Tim trips over it.'
completion_criteria: Every judgment threshold in the reasoning layer enumerated with file:line, what it gates, and a provenance tag (OWNER / CONVENTION / GUESS)
A ranked calibrate-first list by consequence x arbitrariness
testing_criteria: register is accurate to the code (each entry verified against its file:line); no code change so full suite unaffected — 418 green

result.notes: docs/STEWARD_THRESHOLDS.md. Finding: exactly ONE threshold is owner-calibrated (drivelineAttentionWheelHP=450, W-044); the rest are shop convention (drivetrain losses, 30d/500mi windows, coolant 215/235) or — for eleven of them — ungrounded guesses. Top three to calibrate: per-car boost caution (currently a flat 18 psi for every boosted car, and it's a safety caution), the 14/180-day sequence/quiet windows, and the 80-whp engine-internals gate. Recommended structural follow-up: centralize into StewardThresholds with per-constant provenance so a guess can't be added without declaring itself.

items.title: Sourced per-platform live limits — boost caution + air-cooled coolant
items.why: Top calibrate-first item from the W-048 threshold audit. A flat 18 psi boost caution was applied to every boosted car regardless of setup (too high for a supercharged S2000, meaningless for the NA Tundra), and a 215/235F coolant limit was asserted on the air-cooled Baja that has no coolant at all — false authority, exactly what the app forbids.
completion_criteria: PlatformBaseline catalog: per-platform boost caution + air-cooled flag, each entry citing a source (researched from Subaru/Honda/Toyota/VW tuner communities)
OperatingEnvelope.default derives boost caution per-platform (nil if NA) and suppresses coolant limits on air-cooled engines
coolant limits made optional; live rule and Specs editor handle nil
testing_criteria: PlatformBaselineTests: S2000=13, EJ turbo=17, Tundra/Baja boost nil, air-cooled Baja has no coolant limit and emits no coolant observation even at 260F, unknown boosted -> generic 16, every catalog entry cites a source
full suite green

result.notes: Followed my rec (per-car boost caution) grounded in Tim's data (real tuner-site baselines). Sources: subaruforester.org/NASIOC (EJ255 TD04 stock ~12, safe ~16-18); S2KI/Kraftwerks (Rotrex SC ~8-12); 3UR-FE NA 381hp; VW Type-1 air-cooled. Catalog is extensible with a hard rule: a new platform must cite a source or it doesn't belong. Register updated; boost-caution guess retired.

items.title: Owner-calibrate the sequence/quiet windows (Fable concern #3, #2 calibrate-first)
items.why: Three GUESS-tagged reasoning windows from the W-048 audit — invented numbers wearing the authority of facts. Not researchable like the boost baselines; they are Tim usage calls, like the 450 driveline number.
completion_criteria: sequence-lag window owner-set (14 -> 30 days)
quiet-record window owner-set (180 -> 90 days)
flagged-pull timeout replaced with until-resolved (a later clean pull resolves it), no timer
values live in StewardThresholds with Tim/date/rationale provenance
testing_criteria: StewardWindowTests: 20d gap no flag / 40d flags; 80d quiet no / 100d yes; old breach stays, later clean pull resolves, later breach does not
existing PullReportStewardTests updated from the old-timeout assertion
full suite green

result.notes: Tim calibrated: sequence-lag 30d, quiet 90d, flagged pull until-resolved. Started the StewardThresholds home Fable recommended. Noted caveats stand as follow-ups: sequence still keys on a date gap (a 'driven between' signal would be truer) and quiet is one number for a mixed fleet (could be per-car). Explicit pull-dismiss UI is the companion to until-resolved (auto-resolves on a clean pull today).

items.title: Pre-OBD-II awareness — no live-telemetry affordance for the Baja
items.why: Tim flagged that one car (the ~1971 air-cooled VW Baja) predates OBD-II. Offering a 'connect an OBD-II adapter' affordance on a car with no OBD port is false affordance — same class as a coolant limit on an air-cooled engine.
completion_criteria: Vehicle.supportsOBD2 (US: liquid-cooled AND year>=1996; air-cooled classics false)
Live tab shows an honest unavailable notice for a pre-OBD-II car and disables scan; Simulated still works
W-014 driveway checklist excludes the Baja
testing_criteria: PlatformBaselineTests: Baja & a 1991 Civic supportsOBD2 false; S2K/Fozzy/Tundra true
full suite green

result.notes: Same honesty thread as W-049 (air-cooled coolant): the app now knows the Baja has no OBD port and says so rather than offering a connection that can never complete. Heuristic is US-year + cooling; an explicit override can follow if a car ever needs correcting.
